growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron remains one of the clearest AI memory beneficiaries as current coverage points to strong HBM demand and rising memory prices, with the stock jumping ahead of Alphabet earnings on renewed AI capex optimism.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI HBM and tight memory supply make Micron the cheapest high-quality cycle winner in the batch despite volatility.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow’s AI monetization is measurable: Q1 disclosed Now Assist customers spending over $1M in ACV up more than 130% year over year, and today’s Q2 release is the next check on that ramp.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow offers rare overlap of deep drawdown, durable growth, strong FCF, and measurable enterprise AI monetization.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Hasbro posted a clean earnings beat, raised guidance, and showed real momentum in Magic: The Gathering and other core franchises.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Hasbro has tangible earnings momentum, raised guidance, cheap forward valuation, and Magic-driven franchise strength.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: GM raised full-year profit guidance, highlighted defense-unit revenue growth, and still trades as if the EV reset will permanently cap returns.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GM is a cheap cash-flow and buyback story with raised guidance, while the market still over-penalizes the EV reset.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: IREN signed $2.8B of new multi-year AI cloud contracts with leading AI developers and raised its 2026 ARR target above $4B, with roughly 45% of GPU capex covered by customer prepayments.
growth_picks.analyst_take: New $2.8B AI cloud contracts de-risk the pivot, but the balance sheet and capex cycle keep this from being a low-risk compounder.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Atlassian is still about 56% below its 52-week high of $206.00, but the business remains a profitable software franchise on a non-GAAP basis with strong cloud growth, and management has an August 6, 2026 earnings date that can confirm whether the AI and cloud re-acceleration story is real. Bear case: persistent insider selling and GAAP losses leave room for the market to keep treating it as a multiple trap if growth fails to reaccelerate cleanly.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Atlassian offers a rare combination of deep drawdown, strong cloud growth, positive free cash flow, and an August catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Vertiv remains a direct AI power and cooling beneficiary, with recent coverage highlighting NVIDIA co-engineering on liquid cooling and 800V DC power systems while the company’s Q1 results showed 30% sales growth from data-center demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Vertiv is a direct AI data-center power and cooling beneficiary, but the valuation leaves little room for execution misses.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Credo’s FY2026 results showed revenue of $437M, up 157% year over year, confirming that AI-cluster interconnect demand is already translating into outsized monetization.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Credo's AI interconnect growth is real and explosive, but the stock already discounts a lot of that success.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Danaher sold off 11% on July 21, 2026 after a Q2 beat but softer bioprocessing guidance, leaving the stock 28.7% below its 52-week high; the market is punishing one segment miss even though Danaher raised full-year profit guidance and remains a durable, cash-generative healthcare compounder, so the next estimate-reset cycle could mean-revert the move. Bear case: the bioprocessing slowdown is real and the narrower revenue outlook may be the start of a longer de-rating.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Danaher looks like a quality compounder punished too harshly for a bioprocessing reset, creating a reasonable contrarian entry.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Doximity remains more than 70% below its 52-week high after a brutal post-earnings reset, but it is still a profitable platform with roughly 49% non-GAAP net margin and positive free cash flow, and its next earnings call is scheduled for August 6, 2026, giving the selloff a clear re-rating catalyst. Bear case: the weak revenue outlook could prove structural if AI and budget scrutiny cap growth for longer than the market expects.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Doximity is deeply reset, profitable, and cash generative, giving the August earnings catalyst asymmetric upside if guidance stabilizes.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: CEO Anthony Noto has kept buying in the open market, with five 2026 purchases totaling 130,211 shares, while the stock still sits far below prior highs.
growth_picks.analyst_take: SoFi has insider alignment, strong growth, improving profitability, and a near-term earnings catalyst, but upside is capped by valuation and leverage.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The July 15 Department of War awards gave Lockheed a $10.53B USSOCOM global logistics support contract and a $1.60B F-35 initial spares order, which lines up with the July NATO summit's broad push for higher allied defense procurement and sustains a long-duration support and spares backlog.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed has defense-budget tailwinds, fresh contract awards, and a reasonable forward multiple, but execution delays cap conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Northrop Grumman received a July 17 Air Force contract for an enhanced LAIRCM optical detection sensor, and the NATO summit's decision to buy MQ-4C Triton surveillance drones directly highlights demand for Northrop's ISR and air-defense systems as regional instability and air-threat concerns rise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Northrop's raised outlook and record backlog outweigh margin worries after a sizable drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: The White House's July 20 aluminum order lowers the tariff rate for companies investing in U.S. smelters, and Century Aluminum has already said it is planning a new Oklahoma smelter, making it a direct beneficiary of the administration's defense-supply-chain reshoring push.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Century Aluminum is a high-beta reshoring beneficiary with unusually cheap forward valuation and a policy-backed smelter catalyst.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: MP Materials sits at the center of the U.S. rare-earth reshoring effort: the July 20 White House defense-supply-chain order pushes domestic acquisition of critical materials, while recent China export controls on MP and the EU's rare-earth crisis planning keep non-China supply chains in focus.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is strategically valuable rare-earth infrastructure with Pentagon and Apple validation, but poor current profitability keeps this speculative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Aqua Capital, Ltd. accumulated 240,000 Energizer shares across Jul 14, 15, 16, 17, and 20, 2026 for about $4.89M total, a repeated 10% owner build in a $1.37B company that suggests conviction near weakness.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap cash-generating staples supplier with repeated 10% owner buying, but leverage and revenue decline cap conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: EquipmentShare's board approved a $500M share repurchase program on Jul 9, 2026, equal to roughly 11% of its ~$4.3B market cap, which is a meaningful capital-return signal from management.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Raised 2026 outlook plus a $500M buyback provide a real management-confidence signal after a deep drawdown.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: The stock is still more than 22% below its 52-week high after another weak session, but the franchise quality and recovery in revenue and margins keep the setup interesting.
growth_picks.analyst_take: McDonald's remains a high-quality franchise at a reset multiple with an August earnings check and analyst upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Takeda has two regulatory shots on goal, with Oveporexton already under Priority Review and rusfertide carrying a Q3 2026 FDA action date.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Takeda has multiple FDA priority-review catalysts within the near-term window, offset by slower growth and mixed profitability.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Gartner is more than 60% below its 52-week high, but analyst targets still leave room for a rerate if the market decides the selloff went too far.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Gartner is a contrarian cash-flow rerating candidate with deep drawdown, low forward multiple, and squeeze potential into earnings.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Houthi threats in the Red Sea and concurrent Black Sea disruptions are forcing crude tankers to reroute and increasing war-risk premiums, which should raise ton-miles and spot tanker rates for Frontline's crude fleet if the chokepoint stress persists.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Frontline is a cheap crude-tanker cash-flow play on Red Sea and Hormuz disruption, with dividends adding to return potential.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Snowflake signed a $6B AWS collaboration to speed enterprise agentic AI adoption, and its latest quarter grew revenue 30% year over year, making this a real AI platform monetization story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Snowflake has a credible agentic-AI platform story, but the stock is too close to highs and too expensive for a fresh buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Datadog is monetizing the AI observability wave, with Q1 revenue up 32% to $1.006B and full-year guidance raised to $4.3B-$4.34B as AI workloads increase monitoring complexity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Datadog is executing well in AI observability, but valuation and limited target upside argue against chasing.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Multiple insiders bought after the CEO exit, including CFO and legal chief purchases above $500K, which is a high-signal insider cluster in a beaten-up large cap.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: GE Vernova remains a core AI power-infrastructure beneficiary, with backlog and turbine demand still stretched by data-center electrification.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GE Vernova is a premier AI power-infrastructure beneficiary, but backlog enthusiasm and valuation are already very stretched.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Eaton is a direct pick-and-shovel play on data-center electrification, and the 2026 outlook keeps pointing to AI-first power demand.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Eaton is a high-quality electrification winner, but the stock is too close to highs for a value-oriented buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: RTX won a July 17 ground-based radar digitization award worth $309.5M and a July 9 Navy order tied to the MK 45 Capsule Launch System restart, while NATO's summit drive to rearm Europe and expand missile-production capacity keeps air-defense and sensor demand elevated.
growth_picks.analyst_take: RTX has strong defense and commercial aerospace demand, but valuation and proximity to highs make the risk/reward ordinary.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Chevron signed three agreements with Iraq on July 17, including a pipeline investment that creates another export route outside the Strait of Hormuz, so it gains both from new upstream work in Iraq and from the geopolitical premium on alternative oil corridors.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Chevron is a durable energy-security name with Iraq optionality, but current upside is not compelling enough for a fresh buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: GameStop disclosed in its July 2026 Schedule 13D that it bought 3,516,077 eBay shares for $381.3M between Jun 8-15, 2026 and later settled 39,046,658 shares of derivative exposure on Jul 17, turning eBay into a major strategic position.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GameStop's large economic stake and takeover attempt create optionality, but the stock already trades near target and 52-week highs.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Symbotic strengthened its physical-AI platform by acquiring ARMS Innovations on July 2 and keeps a large contracted backlog, giving it a credible multi-year warehouse automation runway.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Warehouse automation catalyst is real, but the valuation is still demanding relative to margins and execution risk.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Asana is trading around $7.60, versus a 52-week high of $15.71, after a year of capitulation, yet FY26 results showed revenue up 9% to $790.8 million, positive non-GAAP operating income, stronger free cash flow, and a fresh $200 million buyback authorization; the next earnings estimate is September 2, 2026. Bear case: GAAP losses are still negative and the turnaround can fail if growth decelerates before the margin gains compound.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Turnaround metrics and free cash flow are improving, but slow growth, GAAP losses, and insider selling argue against upgrading yet.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Workday is trading roughly 43% below its 52-week high after fresh analyst downgrades from Morgan Stanley and CLSA, which is the kind of capitulation that often marks a better entry than the headlines suggest, and its next earnings call is estimated for August 20, 2026. Bear case: AI-driven workflow disruption could be slower to monetize than hoped, and the stock still carries a premium multiple despite the drawdown.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Workday has strong cash flow and a large drawdown, but fresh AI-disruption downgrades and modest target upside keep it in penalty-box status.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Nike remains about 46% below its 52-week high, and the combination of a long drawdown plus turnaround coverage suggests the market may be discounting too much bad news.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nike is washed out enough to watch, but turnaround timing and China/channel weakness still outweigh the valuation reset.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Equifax has been de-rated hard, but UBS still sees mid-20s upside, which is the kind of setup that can drive a contrarian re-rating.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Equifax has rerating potential after a selloff, but the latest quarter was mixed and leverage reduces the margin of safety.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: General Dynamics Electric Boat got a July 9 $255M submarine engineering and planning support modification, which benefits from the same undersea competition and maritime security pressure that is pushing NATO and the U.S. to prioritize submarines, sensors and sea-denial capabilities.
growth_picks.analyst_take: General Dynamics is a high-quality defense compounder with submarine tailwinds, but it is too close to highs for a fresh value entry.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Four City Holding directors - Diane Strong-Treister, Javier Reyes, James Hoyer, and Robert Fisher - bought 110, 83, 95, and 156 shares respectively on Jul 21, 2026 for a combined $59,741, a same-day board cluster in a $1.9B regional bank.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Director buying is constructive, but the regional bank already trades near highs with no analyst upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Adobe is a caution name: Morgan Stanley downgraded it to underweight today, citing AI competition and execution risk as investors question whether generative tools will keep compressing its creative-software moat.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Adobe is cheap for its quality and cash flow, but AI competition and a fresh Morgan Stanley downgrade block a buy call.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Roivant's brepocitinib sits in a Q3 2026 FDA window, and the program already has Priority Review plus supportive Phase 3 data behind it.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Brepocitinib is a credible FDA catalyst, but the stock is near highs and target upside is too limited for a binary-risk buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: UroGen combines a 15.34% short float and 7.63 days to cover with 2026 sales growth momentum, making it one of the cleaner squeeze-style setups in the current screen.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Commercial momentum is real, but the stock sits at highs with negative free cash flow and no target-price upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Independently discovered via web research
catalysts_general: Discovery: Wix is another avoid candidate because current coverage frames prompt-based website builders and vibe-coding tools as direct threats to its drag-and-drop model, even as the stock has already fallen sharply.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Wix looks statistically cheap, but AI disruption, renewed losses, layoffs, and investigation headlines make the drawdown look like a value trap.
